touches.ai_summary: Inbound request from Grayson Weber, Director of CS at VortexEdge. Expressed interest in exploring automation solutions for onboarding workflows. Initial inquiry submitted via website form.

18-minute discovery call with Grayson Weber. Discussed current onboarding challenges at VortexEdge, including manual handoffs and inconsistent customer experience across 38-person CS team. Grayson is new to the Director role (3 months) and looking to establish scalable processes.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can your platform integrate with our existing CRM and ticketing systems?
questions_asked.answer: Typical integration takes 2-3 weeks with our implementation team, supports all major CRMs out of the box

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're planning to scale our customer base by 40% next quarter and current manual processes won't support that growth

next_steps: Grayson to map current onboarding workflow
Rep to send use case examples from PropTech companies

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing three PropTech customer case studies showing 60-70% reduction in onboarding time. Included link to interactive workflow builder demo.

questions_asked.question: Do you have examples specific to companies with 8000+ employees?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, attached case study from BuildCore (12K employees, similar use case)

next_steps: Grayson to review materials and share with team

touches.ai_summary: Email checking in on case study review. Grayson confirmed the BuildCore example resonated strongly with their COO and asked about pricing structure for unlimited automation tier.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical seat count for the unlimited automation package?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers in your segment run 30-50 seats, pricing scales with seat count and automation complexity

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email introducing team members who will join technical evaluation. Grayson mentioned bringing in Sophia Rodriguez (RevOps Analyst) and Lisa Huang (IT Director) for next conversation.
Email sent with technical documentation and security questionnaire ahead of deeper discovery. Requested information about current tech stack and integration requirements.
next_steps: Grayson to complete tech stack survey

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with additional ROI calculator based on VortexEdge's stated team size and growth projections. Grayson responded positively, noting the 18-month payback period aligned with their investment approval process.
next_steps: Finalize agenda for upcoming discovery call

touches.ai_summary: 12-minute discovery call diving deeper into technical requirements and current pain points. Grayson outlined specific automation needs including customer data enrichment, automated task routing, and milestone tracking. Mentioned need to involve procurement (Cameron Kelly and Cameron Huang) before moving to demo stage.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the evaluation process?
questions_asked.answer: Our COO Noah Gomez will need to approve anything over $40K, and procurement will handle contracting

questions_asked.question: Can your platform handle conditional workflows based on customer segment?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our workflow engine supports unlimited conditional logic and segmentation rules

next_steps: Prepare custom workflow examples for VortexEdge use cases

touches.ai_summary: 43-minute product demo focused on onboarding automation workflows. Walked through custom environment configured for VortexEdge's PropTech use case. Demonstrated integration with their existing CRM, automated task routing, and analytics dashboard. Grayson very engaged, took detailed notes.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automated email templates?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable with drag-and-drop editor, supports dynamic variables and A/B testing

questions_asked.question: What level of admin training is required?
questions_asked.answer: 2-day virtual training for admins, plus ongoing office hours and knowledge base access

questions_asked.question: What would success look like in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Automating our top 5 onboarding workflows and reducing manual touchpoints by at least 50%

next_steps: Schedule follow-up demo for technical evaluators
Provide admin portal sandbox access for Sophia

touches.ai_summary: 40-minute technical deep-dive demo with Sophia Rodriguez and Lisa Huang. Originally scheduled for earlier in the week but rescheduled due to conflicting priorities. Covered security architecture, API capabilities, data residency, and SSO integration. Lisa asked detailed questions about infrastructure requirements.

questions_asked.question: Do you support SAML-based SSO with our identity provider?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, SAML 2.0 fully supported, typically configured during implementation

questions_asked.question: What's your SLA for API uptime?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA on enterprise plans, with financial credits for violations

questions_asked.question: Can we export all customer data if we need to migrate in the future?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, full data export via API or bulk CSV download, no lock-in

objections: Need to verify data residency compliance with our EU customers
next_steps: Schedule security review call with InfoSec team

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute hands-on workflow building session with Grayson. Built out a live onboarding workflow together using VortexEdge's actual process map. Grayson tested the interface and expressed confidence in team adoption.

questions_asked.question: How do version control and rollback work for workflows?
questions_asked.answer: Built-in versioning with one-click rollback, plus sandbox environment for testing changes

questions_asked.question: Can we set up approval gates for certain automation steps?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, configurable approval workflows with delegated authority rules

questions_asked.question: Are you ready to move forward with a proposal?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I'll need to loop in Noah and procurement, but I'm confident this solves our needs

next_steps: Schedule exec alignment meeting with Noah Gomez

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email summarizing demo outcomes and confirming next steps. Grayson confirmed internal alignment to move forward and requested pricing proposal for 38 seats with unlimited automation tier.
next_steps: Coordinate exec meeting with Noah's calendar

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with formal proposal outlining $47,900 ACV for 38 seats, unlimited automation package, premium support, and implementation services. Included ROI projection showing $180K savings in Year 1.

15-minute proposal review call with Grayson. Walked through pricing structure, contract terms, and implementation timeline. Grayson comfortable with pricing but flagged need for Noah's approval before proceeding.

questions_asked.question: Is the $47,900 annual or multi-year pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Annual pricing, 10% discount available for 2-year commitment

questions_asked.question: What's Noah's biggest concern likely to be?
questions_asked.answer: Ensuring we can scale this as we grow headcount, he'll want flexibility to add seats

touches.ai_summary: 42-minute exec meeting with Noah Gomez (COO) and Grayson Weber. Presented business case focused on operational efficiency, customer experience improvement, and scalability to support VortexEdge's growth plan. Noah engaged and asked strategic questions about platform roadmap and vendor stability.

questions_asked.question: What's your product development velocity? How often do you ship new features?
questions_asked.answer: Bi-weekly release cycle, major features quarterly, all customers get automatic updates

questions_asked.question: How does seat pricing work if we scale to 60-80 CS reps in the next 18 months?
questions_asked.answer: Tiered volume pricing kicks in at 50+ seats, and we can structure multi-year agreements with growth bands

questions_asked.question: Beyond onboarding, are there other automation opportunities you're considering?
questions_asked.answer: Potentially renewal workflows and health score automation, but onboarding is the priority

objections: Want to ensure contract terms allow for seat expansion without renegotiation
next_steps: Revise proposal to include growth pricing schedule
Address contract flexibility for seat expansion

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Noah and Grayson with revised proposal including tiered seat pricing structure (38 seats initial, pricing locked for up to 75 seats over contract term). Also addressed contract flexibility questions.
next_steps: Schedule follow-up to address any remaining questions

touches.ai_summary: 29-minute negotiation meeting with Noah Gomez. Discussed contract length, payment terms, and implementation timeline. Noah requested annual payment option instead of upfront, and asked about early termination clauses.

questions_asked.question: Can we structure this as quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We typically do annual billing, but can accommodate quarterly with a 5% processing fee

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for getting this approved and signed?
questions_asked.answer: Our budget cycle closes end of next month, ideally signed within 3 weeks

objections: 5% processing fee for quarterly billing feels high, prefer annual payments with 30-day net terms

touches.ai_summary: 35-minute meeting with Noah continuing negotiation discussion. Agreed to annual payment with net-30 terms. Noah raised questions about implementation support and training allocation to ensure smooth rollout.

questions_asked.question: How many implementation hours are included in the package?
questions_asked.answer: 40 hours of implementation support included, additional available at $200/hour if needed

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need more training than the standard 2-day session?
questions_asked.answer: We can add supplemental training sessions, and you'll have access to our online training library

next_steps: Cameron Kelly and Cameron Huang to review legal language

touches.ai_summary: 9-minute quick sync with Noah to confirm final contract details. Noah confirmed internal approval obtained and ready to send to procurement for legal review. Discussed aggressive timeline to close before budget period ends.

questions_asked.question: Any remaining blockers on your side?
questions_asked.answer: No, just need procurement to review standard terms, should be straightforward

next_steps: Send final contract to Cameron Kelly for procurement review

touches.ai_summary: Email to Grayson copying Cameron Kelly and Cameron Huang with final contract documents and redline summary. Outlined agreed terms: $47,900 annual, 38 seats, unlimited automation, net-30 payment, 40 hours implementation.

15-minute negotiation call with Grayson and Cameron Kelly to finalize minor contract language adjustments. Discussed liability caps, data privacy addendum, and termination for convenience clause. Cameron comfortable with standard terms after minor edits.

questions_asked.question: Can we add a mutual NDA reference to the MSA?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can incorporate standard mutual NDA language into Section 8

questions_asked.question: Are we clear to move to signature?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I'll route to Noah for final signature, expect this signed by end of week
